review: AWS ECS runtime-readiness plan set — round 3 go/no-go
process: Four independent read-only reviewers (reality, architecture, quality, systems; general-purpose agents per project doctrine on large plans), decisive findings re-verified against the tree by the orchestrator before synthesis. Findings adjudicated on their own evidence, never by agreement count.
verdict_summary: NO-GO, narrowly. All six round-2 blockers are verified repaired in the plan text, and the plan-internal design is sound across all four lenses. Two new HIGH systemic findings block: both are interactions with UNMODIFIED existing code (app.py mkdir; pre-bind create_app) that the per-plan TDD lens structurally cannot see. Both are cheap plan-text repairs; after them this is a GO.

round2_blocker_repairs.1_request_path_schema_mutation: VERIFIED — plan 11 gates all 6 sites; independent census of every LandscapeDB construction under src/elspeth/web/ confirms the enumeration is exact and complete (architecture + quality + systems each re-censused).
round2_blocker_repairs.2_session_partial_to_stale: VERIFIED — plan 02 text + consumers (03, 04) consistent; underlying invariant confirmed (sessions/schema.py raises on table-set mismatch before shape checks); pinned by tests (soft pin: STALE assertion is in-step, not a named test).
round2_blocker_repairs.3_probe_mkdir_removal: VERIFIED in plan text and strongly pinned by named tests in both doctor and readiness — but see blocking finding B1: an unmodified app.py line defeats the invariant at the web-app layer.
round2_blocker_repairs.4_plan03_dependency_split: VERIFIED — Tasks 1-2 Wave 2, Task 3 Wave 3, dependency tables consistent with overview.
round2_blocker_repairs.5_sink_csv_options_headers_defaults: VERIFIED — field declarations mirror azure_blob_sink.py:178/182 (anchors confirmed); pinned indirectly via minimal-config instantiation tests.
round2_blocker_repairs.6_startup_budget_model: VERIFIED — spec amended to per-probe model (~60s nominal, ~90s two-cold-cluster worst case) — but see blocking finding B2: startPeriod-only guidance is incomplete and too tight.

blocking.title: create_app() unconditional data_dir mkdir defeats the round-2 no-mkdir/unmounted-EFS repair for the running web task
blocking.evidence: src/elspeth/web/app.py:818-819 — settings.data_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) + (data_dir/'runs').mkdir(exist_ok=True), unconditional, before the schema gate. Grep over all 12 plans: no plan touches these lines.
blocking.failure_scenario: EFS present-but-misconfigured (typo'd mountPoint / unattached volume) but container starts; Postgres healthy. create_app creates data_dir in the overlay FS -> schema validation passes -> readiness probes see existing writable dirs -> /api/ready returns 200 READY. Payloads/blobs written during runs land on ephemeral storage and vanish on task recycle. Doctor (pre-traffic) still catches a fresh deploy, but scale-out / auto-recovery / task-replacement events never re-run doctor — the running task's readiness signal is the defense-in-depth layer, and it lies.
blocking.required_fix: In aws-ecs mode create_app must not mkdir the data_dir root: gate app.py:818-819 on deployment_target != DEPLOYMENT_TARGET_AWS_ECS (fail closed if absent, mirroring the doctor/readiness probe policy). Natural owner: plan 04 (owns create_app aws-ecs startup behaviour); add a task with a test asserting aws-ecs startup with a missing data_dir refuses rather than creates.

blocking.title: Schema gate runs pre-socket-bind (~90s closed port); plans size only container startPeriod, never ECS service healthCheckGracePeriodSeconds
blocking.evidence: cli.py:3262-3268 uvicorn.run('elspeth.web.app:create_app', factory=True) — factory calls create_app() during config.load(), before the socket binds; create_app blocks synchronously through the (plan 04) validate-with-retries gate. Grep for grace-period across all plans: zero hits. Spec's own worst case is '~90s plus probe time', so startPeriod=90s is already too tight.
blocking.failure_scenario: Two paused Aurora clusters cold-start serially; healthCheckGracePeriodSeconds unset (effectively 0). Task RUNNING, port closed for ~90s+, ALB health check fails, ECS replaces the task -> relaunch -> same cold start -> restart loop that never converges. The plan's liveness-independence claim only holds after create_app returns.
blocking.required_fix: Plan 10 runbook (+ spec startup-budget section): operators must set BOTH container healthCheck.startPeriod AND service healthCheckGracePeriodSeconds to >= worst-case validate window plus margin (e.g. 150s), with the explanation that the port is closed (not 503) during validation.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: Plan 11 tests use deployment_target='local' but plan 01 defines Literal['default','aws-ecs']
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: s/local/default/ in plan 11 Tasks 1-3 test descriptions; 'local' is the auth_provider vocabulary, not deployment_target. Real WebSettings would raise ValidationError.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: Auth-audit writers are an unwrapped login-time hard dependency on the landscape DB; plan 11 flips them fail-closed without surfacing the posture decision
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: Record the product decision in plan 11: either audit-must-succeed (documented, actionable error instead of raw 500) or graceful degrade matching plans 04/05's (SQLAlchemyError, OSError, SchemaCompatibilityError) posture. auth/routes.py:244 verified unwrapped; blast radius = aws-ecs + local-auth config only.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: Overview claims the 08-before-06/07 merge order makes the security ordering 'structural' — it is prose-only; no test is red on a gateless tree
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: Add a standing guard test to plan 08: if aws_s3 is a registered web-catalog source/sink, validate_pipeline must emit the endpoint_url policy check. That makes the ordering mechanically enforced and un-claims nothing.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: Doctor (the sole --init-schema path) has a single 10s connect_timeout while plan 04 budgets 45s for the same documented Aurora cold start — internal contradiction
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: Extend plan 04's bounded-retry budget to doctor, or add the runbook precondition: set non-zero min ACU before first --init-schema.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: Plan 08's YAML-import/set_source_from_blob route to the validate_pipeline chokepoint is verified by code-reading only
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: One integration test: import YAML carrying aws_s3+endpoint_url -> assert persisted state is_valid=False. This is the one web-authorship vector without tool-level defense-in-depth.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: Readiness failures/timeouts are never logged (body discarded by ALB; _drain suppresses the abandoned future's exception)
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: One structured log line naming the failed/timed-out probe (class-only detail, no secrets) in readiness.py failure paths.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: S3 IfNoneMatch conditional-put is exercised only against a hand-rolled fake; the race-free overwrite=False acceptance criterion is asserted, not proven
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: Acknowledge in writing in plan 07 (accepted residual) or add the optional LocalStack test to the follow-ups.

should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.title: Postgres/Aurora has no schema-epoch backstop (epoch rides SQLite PRAGMA user_version; database.py:1004 hardcodes 0 for non-sqlite)
should_fix_in_same_repair_pass.fix: State the limitation in plan 02/spec: on Aurora, drift detection is structural-only; a semantics-only schema revision validates CURRENT. PG version table = future work.

record_only.title: AST write-gate is syntactic/name-based (aliased import evades) — adequate as a regression seal, not a proof (architecture+quality convergent)
pg_advisory_lock wait is unbounded — add lock_timeout + 'another init in progress' message
256MiB exact-boundary and empty-object cases unpinned in plan 06 tests
Plans 04/05 both edit app.py:293-294; order assumed in prose (05 after 04), not hardened
readiness _check_landscape builds+disposes a pooled engine per cache miss; session probe shares the live session pool
Session-STALE doctor message should mention 'if init was interrupted, rerun --init-schema' before advising drop/recreate
readiness.py module-global ThreadPoolExecutor created at import, never disposed
Overview prose says extras 'web,...'; real extra is 'webui' (plan 10 is correct)
AwsS3Source vs AWSS3Sink casing split — already tracked as overview follow-up 4; plan 06 test/snapshot rename ripple lands on the reconciliation owner
±1-2 line drift in a few anchors (app.py 818 vs 819, 875, 176; service.py 1599) — symbol-named, self-correcting

positive_verifications: Plan 11's six-site enumeration independently re-censused by three reviewers against every LandscapeDB construction under src/elspeth/web/ — exact and complete; the AST guard lands green as claimed (given plan 04 lands first, which plan 11's Depends-on declares).

Fail-closed model verified end-to-end against the repo's LIVE epoch-21->22 drift in data/runs/audit.db: routing_events.run_id is in _REQUIRED_COLUMNS (database.py:296) -> DIVERGENT->STALE -> startup refuses -> doctor advises drop/recreate.
pg_advisory_lock serialization pinned by a real testcontainer test asserting a pg_locks row; double-checked locking correct (re-probe inside the lock).

Reality spot-checks: 100% hit rate on dozens of code anchors including all six plan-11 write sites, database.py:624-626/1385-1390 internals, chat_solver.py:444, azure_blob_sink.py:178/182, validate_pipeline chokepoint service.py:665-667.
Round-2 mis-rejected chat_solver warning now owned by plan 08 Task 4 with correct anchor, sequenced after 06 + 08 Tasks 1-3.
next_steps: Repair the two blocking findings in plan text (B1: new plan-04 task gating app.py:818-819; B2: plan-10 runbook + spec amendment), fold in S1-S8 where cheap, then this set is GO without a further full-panel round — a single verification pass over the amended sections suffices.
repairs_applied: 2026-07-08, same session: B1 (plan 04 Task 2 gates the create_app mkdir, fail-closed, + require_data_dir_mounted + 3 tests), B2 (spec Operational Budgets amended with the closed-port/pre-bind model and both ECS knobs at ~150s; plan 05 ops doc + plan 10 runbook updated), S1-S8, and the cheap LOW fold-ins (lock_timeout + test, 256MiB boundary/empty-object pins, session-STALE message, Wave-2 04-before-05 order, plan 05 accepted residuals, AST-guard boundary note, webui typo). Itemized mapping in the overview's 'Third review round' record. Not re-fixed by design: AwsS3Source/AWSS3Sink casing (follow-up 4), readiness engine-per-miss / shared session pool / executor lifetime (recorded as accepted residuals in plan 05).
